TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-29-2013 IN 504, PLAINTIFF TRIBECA COMPANIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WITH RESPECT TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THERE REMAINS AN ISSUE FOR THE TRIER OF FACT WHETHER THE $1,000,000 TRANSMITTED INTO TRIBECA'S ESCROW BY A THIRD PARTY PRIOR TO THE CREATION OF THE ESCROW AGREEMENT REFERENCING DEPOSITED FUNDS CONSTITUTED FUNDS "DEPOSITED BY" TRIBECA WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE AGREEMENT GIVEN ALL THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE CASE. ONE ORDER SIGNED AS TO BOTH MOTIONS HEARD TODAY. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED (504)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY HEARING SET FOR FEB-15-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO TRIBECA AND SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO TRIBECCA AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO SKY PACIFIC HOLDINGS. THE FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED AS TO TRIBECCA. AS TO THE SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION, TRIBECA IS GRANTED LEAVE TO PLEAD HOW THE REPRESENTATION WAS MADE, BY WHOM, AND THAT THE REPRESENTER HAD AUTHORITY TO SPEAK. AS TO THE FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SKY PACIFIC HOLDINGS IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING IT HAS STANDING TO ASSERT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT AMERICAN TITLE. AS TO THE SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SKY PACIFIC HOLDINGS IS GRANTED LEAVE TO PLEAD A MISREPRESENTATION MADE TO IT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

REPLY MEMO P/A IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO SKY PACIFIC'S FOURTH, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND TRIBECA'S SEVENTH AND EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SKY PACIFIC'S SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. SKY PACIFIC HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS SHOWING IT HAS STANDING TO BRING THE FOURTH, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT AMERICAN TITLE. SKY PACIFIC FAILED TO ALLEGE A MISREPRESENTATION MADE TO IT AND THUS ITS SEVENTH AND EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DEFICIENT AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THIS DEFICIENCY CAN BE CURED. TRIBECA'S SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-29-2013 IN 504, DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO SKY PACIFIC, WHICH IS A STRANGER TO THE ESCROW AGREEMENT AND, AT MOST, AN INDIRECT BENEFICIARY. NEITHER TRIBECA'S UNEXPRESSED PURPOSES NOR COMMUNICATIONS SUBSEQUENT TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THE ESCROW CAN CREATE A PROTECTIBLE INTEREST FOR SKY PACIFIC. THE MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. THERE REMAINS AN ISSUE FOR THE TRIER OF FACT WHETHER THE $1,000,000 TRANSMITTED INTO TRIBECA'S ESCROW BY A THIRD PARTY PRIOR TO THE CREATION OF THE ESCROW AGREEMENT REFERENCING DEPOSITED FUNDS CONSTITUTED FUNDS "DEPOSITED BY" TRIBECA WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE AGREEMENT GIVEN ALL THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE CASE. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT TRIBECA CANNOT PROVE SOME INJURY ARISING FROM TRIBECA'S LOSS OF CONTROL OVER THE ESCROWED FUNDS. ONE ORDER SIGNED AS TO BOTH MOTIONS HEARD TODAY. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED (504)
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF SKY PACIFIC HOLDINGS I, LLC TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

Defendant First American Title Insurance Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Horace W. Green of Green & Humbert (415.837.5433) for plaintiffs and responding parties; Michael Fogarty of Boutin Jones, Inc. (916.321.4444) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion is granted with respect to Sky Pacific, which is a stranger to the escrow agreement and, at most, an indirect beneficiary. Neither Tribeca's unexpressed purposes nor communications subsequent to the establishment of the escrow can create a protectible interest for Sky Pacific. The motion is otherwise denied. There remains an issue for the trier of fact whether the $1,000,000 transmitted into Tribeca's escrow by a third party prior to the creation of the escrow agreement referencing deposited funds constituted funds "deposited by" Tribeca within the meaning of the agreement given all the circumstances of the case. Defendant has not shown that Tribeca cannot prove some injury arising from Tribeca's loss of control over the escrowed funds. One order signed as to both motions heard today. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Plaintiffs Tribeca Companies, Llc, Sky Pacific Holdings I, Llc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF TRIBECA COMPANIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WITH RESPECT TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT

Appearances:
Horace W. Green of Green & Humbert (415.837.5433) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Michael Fogarty of Boutin Jones, Inc. (916.321.4444) for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. There remains an issue for the trier of fact whether the $1,000,000 transmitted into Tribeca's escrow by a third party prior to the creation of the escrow agreement referencing deposited funds constituted funds "deposited by" Tribeca within the meaning of the agreement given all the circumstances of the case. One order signed as to both motions heard today. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Defendant First American Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Horace W. Green of Green & Humbert (837.5433) for plaintiff and responding party; Jack Praetzellis of MBV Law LLP (781.4400 x1424) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Overruled as to Sky Pacific's fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action and Tribeca's seventh and eight causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to Sky Pacific's seventh and eighth causes of action. Sky Pacific has alleged sufficient facts showing it has standing to bring the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action against Defendant American Title. Sky Pacific failed to allege a misrepresentation made to it and thus its seventh and eight causes of action are deficient and it does not appear that this deficiency can be cured. Tribeca's seventh and eighth causes of action are adequately pled. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dwight C. Donovan, Esq., of MBV Law LLP, Tel. 415.781.4400 x1463, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Horace W. Green, Esq., of Green & Humbert, Tel. 415.837.5433, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action as to Tribeca and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the seventh and eighth causes of action as to Tribecca and all causes of action as to Sky Pacific Holdings. The fourth, fifth and sixth claims are adequately pled as to Tribecca. As to the seventh and eighth causes of action, Tribeca is granted leave to plead how the representation was made, by whom, and that the representer had authority to speak. As to the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action, Sky Pacific Holdings is granted leave to allege facts showing it has standing to assert these causes of action against Defendant American Title. As to the seventh and eighth causes of action, Sky Pacific Holdings is granted leave to plead a misrepresentation made to it. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
